review.note: Bare abstains resolved by review: deterministic findings + dropped_flags kept verbatim (both empty); CONFIRMED-CORRECT claims dropped with auditable advisory:claims-auditor notes; genuine concerns -> advisory:*; significance -> subjective:*. Internal numbers recomputed clean before any advisory.

routed_to_human.note: T4 over-reach on the word 'optimal'. The 1% total doping (0.5% Zn + 0.5% formate) sums correctly, but 'optimal' asserts a demonstrated optimum over a doping series. The ClaimGraph carries only the single 1% point (ev3); without the doping-vs-PLQY/lifetime sweep (the other concentrations tried and their metrics) this is 'best of what was tested', not a proven optimum. What would confirm it: the full dose-response series showing 1% beats neighbouring concentrations on the headline metrics.
routed_to_human.quote:
the optimal
total doping concentration of zinc ions (Zn(II)) and formate anions (Fo−) is determined to
be 1% relative to Pb(II).

routed_to_human.note: T4 scope mismatch between two stability statements made together. 'No yellowing after >1 year' is shelf/dark stability of laminated films, whereas the paper's own data show 'permanent photodegradation after 500 hrs' under 532 nm partial illumination (1-5 suns). Reporting the favourable shelf-life alongside an operational-stability failure risks a reader conflating the two. What would confirm robustness: continuous full-area operational/MPP stability under 1-sun AM1.5 with a quantified retained-performance %.
routed_to_human.quote:
laser irradiation with 532 nm through a pinhole
or a slit (=partial illumination) showed clear signs of permanent photodegradation after
500 hrs

routed_to_human.note: Field plausibility (perovskite optoelectronics). Wide-bandgap triple-halide (I/Br/Cl) perovskites are known to undergo light-induced halide segregation (the Hoke effect), which is the textbook origin of exactly the 532 nm photodegradation observed here. A claim that 1% Zn/formate doping suppresses this needs direct evidence (e.g. PL-peak stability vs illumination time, or XRD showing no phase split), not just a 'no yellowing in the dark' observation. Genuinely not code-settleable from the extracted numbers.
routed_to_human.quote:
laser irradiation with 532 nm through a pinhole
or a slit (=partial illumination) showed clear signs of permanent photodegradation after
500 hrs

routed_to_human.note: The 44% theoretical / ~41% practical / 35.0% state-of-art chain is internally consistent (its 35.0% matches the LONGi record in c6), and 44% is a reasonable 2-terminal Si/perovskite radiative limit. The weak link is the '~41% practical' ceiling: it carries no citation or derivation in the graph (ev21 is prose only) and reads as a forward-looking projection rather than a sourced figure. What would confirm it: a cited practical-limit study or an explicit loss-budget calculation.
routed_to_human.quote:
there
remains significant room for improvement towards the theoretical limit of 44%.

routed_to_human.note: Whether obtaining 14% PLQY and a 6.84 us lifetime at 1% Zn/formate doping is an important or novel advance for wide-bandgap perovskite tandems — vs an incremental improvement over the Mariotti baseline (3.75% PLQY, ~2 us) — is a significance/novelty-worth judgment. Surfaced, explicitly not scored.
routed_to_human.quote:
A PLQY
of 14% has been obtained and a PL lifetime of 6.84 µs.

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach. 14% absolute PLQY on quartz is stated identically in abstract, Results (Fig 5, PLQY=14%), and Conclusions — internally consistent. Skeptic/methodologist: a single absolute-PLQY value with no spread is the weakest link, but that is a reporting-completeness note, not an over-claim. no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
Photoluminescence quantum yields
(up to 14% on quartz), as well as photoluminescence lifetimes (up to ~7 µs on quartz),
indicate improved optical properties.

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach. Abstract '~7 us' faithfully rounds the Fig-6 headline lifetime of 6.84 us (delta 0.16 us, well within the +-1.1 us fit uncertainty); the '~' is appropriately hedged. no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote: photoluminescence lifetimes (up to ~7 µs on quartz)

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach. T1 fixed external values: SQ single-junction limit 33.7% (detailed balance near the optimal ~1.34 eV gap, AM1.5G) and the silicon intrinsic/Auger (Richter) limit 29.4% are both textbook-correct and correctly ordered (33.7 > 29.4 < 100). no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
capped at 33.7% by the Shockley–Queisser (SQ) limit [1]
and, considering Auger recombination [2], realistically limited to 29.4% for silicon (Si).

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach. '~45% theoretical PCE for tandems' is an approximate, explicitly cited (ref [3]) value consistent with the 2-terminal Si/perovskite radiative-limit literature (~44-45%); the '~' is hedged. no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote: theoretically enabling PCEs up to ~45% [3]

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach BY THE PAPER. T5 external record: LONGi 35.0% certified perovskite-Si tandem is a correctly attributed, widely-reported value. Independent confirmation needs a certified-record lookup (records are time-sensitive). no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote: The current certified world record obtained by LONGi stands at 35.0% PCE

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach. T5 citation: Mariotti et al. 32.5% certified triple-halide perovskite-Si tandem (2022) is correctly attributed; the value and year match the known result. Independent confirmation needs the cited source. no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
Mariotti et al. [6] achieved a certified efficiency of 32.5% in
triple-halide perovskite–silicon tandem solar cells in 2022.

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach. T5 citation: Jeong et al., 2% formate -> 25.2% cell efficiency, is reported as someone else's result with the dopant level attached; faithful attribution. Independent confirmation needs the cited source. no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
the addition of 2% (Fo−) formate anions
significantly increases cell efficiency to 25.2%

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach. T5 citation: 27.2% single-junction record (ref [31]) is correctly attributed. Independent confirmation needs the cited source. no numeric verifier covers it yet.

the current

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach. T5 citation: 34.58% certified Si tandem via asymmetric carbazole HTL (ref [16]) is faithfully attributed. Independent confirmation needs the cited source. no numeric verifier covers it yet.

resulting in a Si

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach. Comparative claim (this work 14% PLQY vs Mariotti 3.75% on quartz): both numbers appear elsewhere in the graph (ev1/ev13), 14 > 3.75 holds, and the paper states 'higher' rather than a specific multiple — no inflated ratio. no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
a maximum PLQY of 14% was
obtained for wide-bandgap perovskite on quartz

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach. T5 citation: Mariotti 3.75% PLQY and ~2 us (tail-fit) on quartz are reported as the comparator's values with the fit method named; faithful. Independent confirmation needs the cited source. no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
Mariotti et al. [6] reported 3.75% as PLQY for their films on
quartz (and a PL lifetime of ~2 µs, by tail-fit)

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach. Lifetimes 6.84 / 3.65 / 1.57 us match Fig-6 inset (ev14) exactly, each with its own uncertainty; the 2025>2024>2019 ordering is internally consistent. Domain-expert: a 14% PLQY paired with a ~6.84 us lifetime is physically plausible for a wide-bandgap mixed-halide film (trap-limited but radiatively slow); tightening this would need the radiative rate k_r, which the paper does not report. no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
the red
trace shows our 2025 material with a lifetime of 6.84 ± 1.1 µs, compared to 3.65 ± 0.8 µs
(2024, blue) and 1.57 ± 0.1 µs (2019, green)

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach. T0 stretched-exponential beta values 0.90 / 0.77 / 0.84 all lie in the physical (0,1] range and are paired with the matching lifetimes; the non-monotonic betas are normal for heterogeneous decay. no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
Beta values are, respectively, 0.90 (1.57 µs) [9], 0.77 (3.65 µs) [11], and 0.84 (6.84 µs),
obtained with stretched exponential fitting.

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach. 'ca. 400 nm' crystal dimension is an explicitly approximate SEM read (Fig 4 left); the 'ca.' hedges it and no exact value is asserted. no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
The perovskite crystals have a dimension of
ca. 400 nm (see Figure 4—left).

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach. T5 citation: Mariotti 25% loss after 478 h (undoped) is reported as the comparator's degradation figure with its exact hours; faithful attribution. Independent confirmation needs the cited source. no numeric verifier covers it yet.

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach on the NUMBERS. Conclusions restate PLQY 14% and lifetime 6.84 us at 1% doping, both consistent with Results (ev1/ev2). Whether this constitutes a 'significant' advance is routed separately as subjective:significance. no numeric verifier covers it yet.
routed_to_human.quote:
A PLQY
of 14% has been obtained and a PL lifetime of 6.84 µs.

routed_to_human.note: Reviewed, no over-reach. T0 experimental parameters (5000 rpm, 45 s, 300 uL anisole at 25 s, 100 C / 20 min anneal) are a procedural recipe; nothing to recompute. no numeric verifier covers it yet.
